notes: Single no-op turn in a LIVE pi REPL session, asserting that the dashboard shows the model the agent was launched with — ModelName != 'unknown', ContextWindow matching the model's published window. WHERE THE MODEL CLAIM LIVES: pi records the active model on each assistant message at message.model (parser.go L104) — and on model_change events at modelId (L41) — which the pi parser pulls through tailer.NormalizeModelName into ev.ModelName, populating SessionMetrics.ModelName from turn 1. That metadata is METRICS-level: the expected.jsonl phase DSL (validate/expected.go Phase struct: phase / expected_state / kind / max_delay_ms / duration_at_least_ms / same_session_as / new_session / invariants) operates over the lifecycle events.jsonl stream, which carries NO model_name field — so the model identification itself is NOT expressible as a phase here. It is covered by the pi parser unit tests (extractPiModel from message.model) and surfaces in the replay golden's summary.model_name; per issue #440 the concrete model string is environment-dependent (whatever model pi boots on), so the metrics-level assertion is 'a model_name IS present (non-empty, not unknown)', not a hardcoded value. expected.jsonl therefore asserts only the standard basic-turn lifecycle skeleton: ready→working→ready, no waiting, a single process_exited. WHY interactive (not headless pi --print): per concern C6 the headless single-shot driver exits too fast and drifts; the interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) keeps the rollout REPL alive via send+wait_turn and tears it down once at the end. C7 / SESSION-IDENTITY: the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch (it bridges the brief gap until the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears), so session_birth — which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 2s of launch' — legitimately matches that pre-session. The TURN ARC must land on the UUID rollout session: turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the rollout session (the pre-session never goes working); turn_end, pid_bind, and teardown then pin to turn_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, the concrete model name, and sub-second timing vary (model-output / environment variance). Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on a single rollout session_id, never entering waiting, a single process_exited, and a final ready state.

text: A session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
User prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the pre-session never enters working)
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session
REPL torn down once after the turn → a single process_exited on the rollout session; state immediately before teardown is ready
